profile.name: Muhammad Subhan Rauf
profile.headline: I build LLM systems that survive production.
profile.hero_support_text: AI/ML engineer from Pakistan. I've shipped an AI receptionist that books real medical appointments over WhatsApp, fine-tuned a 7B model on a single GPU, and decoded digits from brain signals. Currently open to AI Engineer roles.
profile.about_text:
Computer Science graduate from FAST-NUCES. I got into AI through the backend door — not demos, but the unglamorous parts that make LLM systems trustworthy: idempotent webhooks, guardrails, traces, fallbacks, tests. The engineer I'm trying to become builds things that are boringly reliable.

Currently: open to AI/ML Engineer roles, on-site or remote.

projects.title: Clinic AI: WhatsApp Healthcare Receptionist
projects.one_liner: An AI receptionist that answers patients on WhatsApp and books appointments directly into a clinic's real calendar.
projects.case_study:
Private clinics lose front-desk hours to phone calls. Clinic AI answers patient queries and books appointments end-to-end over WhatsApp, writing into the Cliniko practice-management system.

**How it works:** A LangGraph supervisor classifies each incoming message and routes it to specialized sub-agents (booking, FAQ, escalation) through conditional edges, with per-conversation state checkpointed per WhatsApp number. Incoming webhooks are made idempotent with atomic Redis operations, because WhatsApp gateways redeliver — and a duplicate here means a double-booked patient. NeMo Guardrails screen every input and output before the model or the patient sees it. Every turn is traced in Langfuse: spans for tool calls, token counts, and cost per conversation.

**The decision I'd defend in an interview:** when Redis is unreachable, the system fails open — a rare duplicate booking is recoverable; a receptionist that goes silent for every patient is an outage.
projects.stack_line: LangGraph · FastAPI · Redis · NeMo Guardrails · Langfuse · Cliniko API · WhatsApp (WAHA) · Docker

projects.title: QLoRA Fine-Tune of Mistral-7B
projects.one_liner: Fine-tuned a 7B-parameter model on a custom entry-test dataset — on a single free-tier GPU.
projects.case_study:
Full fine-tuning of a 7B model needs ~60GB of VRAM. I had a fraction of that.

**How it works:** The frozen base is stored in 4-bit NF4 with double quantization and dequantized on the fly for compute; rank-16 LoRA adapters (α=32) attach to all seven attention and MLP projection layers, so under 1% of parameters actually train. Batch size 1 with 8-step gradient accumulation simulates a real batch; a paged 8-bit AdamW optimizer absorbs memory spikes. Trained with SFTTrainer on Mistral's [INST] format, model selection by validation loss.

projects.title: EEG Brain-Computer Interface
projects.one_liner: Classifying which digit you're looking at — from brain signals.
projects.case_study:
My final-year project: an SSVEP-based BCI. Each digit flickers at a distinct frequency; the visual cortex entrains to the one you attend to, encoding your choice in the EEG's frequency signature.

**How it works:** 3-second EEG windows at 250Hz are Z-score normalized (scaler persisted so inference matches training exactly) and fed to a lightweight 1D-CNN — two Conv1D blocks learning temporal filters, dropout-regularized, softmax over 10 digits. The network is deliberately small so a forward pass meets a real-time latency budget, driving a live web dashboard.

projects.title: YouTube Video Q&A (RAG)
projects.one_liner: Chat with any YouTube video — answers grounded strictly in its transcript.
projects.case_study: **How it works:** The transcript is split by a recursive character splitter (1000 chars, 200 overlap) so semantic units survive chunking, embedded with MiniLM into a FAISS index, and retrieved with MMR — maximal marginal relevance — so the top-3 chunks are relevant *and* diverse instead of near-duplicates. Mistral-7B answers at temperature 0.1 under a prompt that forces 'I don't know' when the context is insufficient. Built twice: once manually, once as an LCEL pipeline with parallel context/question branches — same behavior, composable graph.
projects.stack_line: LangChain · FAISS · sentence-transformers · Streamlit · Hugging Face

projects.title: F1 Race Outcome Prediction
projects.one_liner: Predicting race finishing order from telemetry — and learning where small-data ML breaks.
projects.case_study: An academic pipeline project: sector times and qualifying pace pulled via FastF1, gradient-boosted trees predicting race time per driver, drivers ranked by prediction, evaluated with MAE on a held-out split. The honest lesson it taught me: with 14 rows, evaluation is fragile and leakage hides easily — which is exactly why I now treat validation design as seriously as model choice.

experience.description: Built the backend for an AI-driven therapeutic system: FastAPI services integrating OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Hugging Face models behind one unified layer with routing and fallback — so a provider outage degrades gracefully instead of taking the product down. Implemented the RAG framework, orchestrated workflows with LangChain/LangGraph, and monitored token usage, cost, and latency in LangSmith. As Team Manager, coordinated the intern cohort's delegation, progress, and supervisor communication.
